page: Amazon Web Services c6in.2xlarge is a compute-optimized server instance featuring 8 vCPUs and 16.0 GB of DDR4 memory. Powered by an Intel Xeon 8375C processor running at 3.5 GHz, this x86_64 architecture server provides 4 dedicated physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. The processor includes 54 MB of L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 for vector processing and SHA-NI for cryptographic acceleration. It operates on the AWS Nitro hypervisor and does not include local storage or GPU hardware.

Performance benchmarks position this instance in the average tier for most compute and memory operations. It achieves a Geekbench single-core score of 1501.0 and a multi-core score of 5599.0, alongside a Passmark CPU score of 11053.75. Memory bandwidth is measured at 412.8 GB/sec for cached small blocks and 48.61 GB/sec for uncached large blocks. While it handles Redis SET operations at 7.93 million operations per second and static web serving at 12.28 GB/sec, it exhibits weaker performance in bzip3 decompression and medium-sized 7B LLM inference tasks.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the c6in.2xlarge offers a balanced compute-to-memory ratio of 1:2, making it a resource-dense option for compute-bound workloads that do not require massive memory footprints or local storage. With a baseline network bandwidth of 12 Gbps, this instance is optimized for network-bound and EBS-reliant tasks. It is suitable for workloads such as web serving, database operations, and moderate computational tasks.
description: Amazon Web Services c6in.2xlarge is a compute-optimized instance featuring an Intel Xeon 8375C processor with 8 vCPUs and 16.0 GB of DDR4 memory. Operating on the Nitro hypervisor, this x86_64 server provides 4 physical cores at 3.5 GHz and supports AVX-512 and cryptographic acceleration. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPUs, relying instead on network-attached storage and a 12 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Benchmark data shows average performance across standard compute tasks, with a Passmark CPU score of 11053.75 and Redis SET throughput of 7.93 million operations per second. However, it displays weaker capabilities in multi-threaded bzip3 decompression and medium-model LLM inference. This instance is qualitatively cost-efficient for compute-heavy, network-optimized workloads such as static web serving, database operations, and distributed processing.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c6in.2xlarge is a compute-optimized server with 8 vCPUs, 16 GB DDR4 RAM, and an Intel Xeon 8375C processor. It offers 12 Gbps network bandwidth and average compute performance.

tagline: An x86_64 compute-optimized configuration featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processors and high-speed network performance for compute-bound workloads.
